name: Case Study - Joyce James shares a photographic journey

step.name: Why did you want to become a photographer?
step.text: I've enjoyed taking photographs for many years – and initially I just wanted to improve. I guess that is still what drives me – but the desire to improve has taken on a much deeper meaning!

step.name: Were you scared or anxious to start out?
step.text: No – ignorance is bliss! I had no idea at the outset that it would become such a big part of my life.

step.name: Why did you select Alan as your tutor?
step.text: I bought a Groupon voucher – on a whim. View fullsize An early attempt Sep 2013

step.name: What was your main motivation to commit to Alan’s course?
step.text: I was hooked at the Introductory Session. I came home buzzing and couldn't wait to do the next one. I've been like that ever since!

step.name: How do you feel Alan’s teaching style has helped develop your skills the most?
step.text: He has a very direct and honest style and he completely respects my intelligence – he doesn't do flannel or give false praise. That suits me – he tells me quite candidly what is right or wrong with my efforts and I listen and do my best to learn. He sets high expectations and can be quite demanding – but that's like an unspoken agreement between us. I want to be as good as I can be and he wants that too. I like that he sets the bar high. I should also stress that Alan has a lively sense of humour and the sessions are good fun - we get on well. It wouldn’t work otherwise.

step.name: What are the key things Alan has helped you with?
step.text: I was a complete novice and so I needed to learn all aspects of photography – technical know how, composition, the importance of light – you name it, I'm learning about it! View fullsize

step.name: How do you think you’ve grown as a photographer?
step.text: I’d say I probably scored 1/10 in terms of ability when I started out almost 2 years ago. I'm probably at 6/10 now. I still have a lot to learn. But that's good – if it was easy it wouldn't be nearly as much fun.

step.name: Could you describe how you felt when you were featured in the intermediates photography exhibition?
step.text: The Opening Night was amazing - my family and friends turned out to support me and I felt proud, excited and somewhat emotional. The exhibition itself was the culmination of a lot of hard work – I was also delighted and relieved that I'd succeeded in getting my panel ready.

step.name: What are your ambitions for the future with photography?
step.text: I don't know – I wouldn't like to put a ceiling on my ambition. I have no intentions of making a career out of it or anything like that – but I certainly intend to be a lot better at it than I am now. View fullsize My eye has developed and subject range is much broader than it used to be

step.name: What advice would you give others who are thinking of starting photography tuition or who are currently studying?
step.text: I’m not sure that I want to give advice – everyone goes into it for different reasons and with different outcomes in mind and that's good. We're all different after all. Just enjoy it I guess. View fullsize Recent work Photography Tuition comes in all shapes and sizes - you may be one of those who prefers practical or theory - you may be someone who can't attend classes in the Midlands or in the evenings. You may live abroad or just have limited time or money. Whatever your constraints remember that I can provide a varied package of photography tuition to suit anyone's needs and budgets. Le...
